channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The API Design Lesson I Learned From Building Nectarine
item.description: One of the most surprising lessons I've learned while building software is that APIs are not really about code. They're about communication. At first, that sounds strange. After all, APIs exist so...

item.title: This video bird feeder is the surprise hit of Prime Day
item.description: I can understand why The Verge readers are flocking to the BirdBuddy deal we posted at the beginning of Prime Day. The BirdBuddy Pro with Solar Panels is a wholesome upgrade for your yard, but the...

item.title: What does AWS Certification mean to me as a long-time developer?
item.description: After six months of studying, the day had finally arrived. On a Tuesday morning at 7:45 AM, I started the check-in process for my AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate exam. I took the...

item.title: Guardrails: Keeping Your AI Agent From Going Off the Rails
item.description: Hello, I'm Maneshwar. I'm building git-lrc, a Micro AI code reviewer that runs on every commit. It is free and source-available on Github. Star git-lrc to help devs discover the project. Do give...

item.title: Why everyone from OpenAI to SpaceX is building their own chips (and turning up the heat on Nvidia)
item.description: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending. OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built...

item.title: Organize your agents into workspaces by domain
item.description: The first agent you give an inbox to is easy. You set a send cap, maybe a spam policy, attach a couple of inbound rules, and you're done. The trouble starts at the tenth agent. And the fiftieth....

item.title: Stop building backends for simple contact forms 🛑
item.description: Tired of spinning up a Node server or wrestling with PHP just to make your static site's "Contact Me" form work? I built CodeFreeForm to fix exactly that. It's a completely free, serverless API...

item.title: The Agentic Stack: Discover the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.
item.description: Learn how to move from building single agents to managing a mission control of thousands of them – all with complete governance. The Agentic Stack: Why Managing Thousands of AI Agents Is the Next...

item.title: Building a Zero-Leak Postgres MCP Gateway in Go
item.description: The promise of agentic AI workflows introduces a critical architectural paradox: to make an LLM deeply useful, you must grant it structural awareness of your data layer. Traditional integration...

item.title: GenAI Isn't Just for Product Teams
item.description: Most GenAI use cases today focus on product teams. Build a customer chatbot. Generate marketing copy. Develop a new product feature. But DevOps, Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), and Cloud...

item.title: I built a lightweight jQuery Tree Select plugin 🌳
item.description: While working on HTML/jQuery projects, I needed a simple way to create a tree-style dropdown select without adding heavy dependencies. So I built JqTreeSelect — a lightweight jQuery plugin for...

item.title: Vibe Coding Is Not Software Development — And It's Starting to Show
item.description: A few weeks ago, a colleague came to me excited about a side project he'd been building. He'd been using Claude to generate the entire codebase — no prior development experience, just prompts and...

item.title: It’s the last day of Prime Day — here are over 140 great deals to choose from
item.description: We’ve arrived at the final day of Prime Day, which at this point should probably be called “Prime Week.” We’ve found discounts on all manner of gadgets, including TVs, smart home tech, chargers,...

item.title: OpenAI Has New AI Models. Here’s Why You Can’t Use Them
item.description: The White House asked OpenAI to delay the rollout of its GPT-5.6 AI models, two weeks after Anthropic had to take its most advanced AI models offline.

item.title: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.6 amid US AI regulatory drama
item.description: Less than 24 hours after news broke that OpenAI would stagger its next model release at the request of the Trump administration, that model, GPT-5.6, is here. On Friday, the company unveiled the...

item.title: Smart lock maker Level has been gutted and its founders are out
item.description: Assa Abloy has laid off the majority of staff at Level Home, the smart lock company known for building smart tech into traditional-looking deadbolts, and is folding the business into Kwikset,...

item.title: Meta Is Testing Facial Recognition for Police and Military
item.description: We know that ICE wants to deploy eyeglasses with facial recognition that can identify people in real time. Turns out Meta is prototyping the feature with a Pentagon supplier. (Alternate news story.)

item.title: You won’t have long to get these iPad deals before Apple’s price hike
item.description: Apple just raised prices across its iPad and MacBook lineup. The good news is that many retailers are still selling their inventory at the old prices or far less, which means you can still score...

item.title: It’s not about Anthropic vs. OpenAI anymore
item.description: AI models have progressed to the point where their capabilities have real political consequences. Dealing with those consequences will require collective action.

item.title: Chinese-Speaking APT Deploys New TinyRCT Backdoor in Southeast Asia Campaign
item.description: A Chinese-speaking advanced persistent threat (APT) actor has been linked to a new custom backdoor called TinyRCT as part of cyber attacks aimed at government entities and critical infrastructure...

item.title: The 16 best robot vacuum deals you can still get before Prime Day ends
item.description: If you’ve been wanting to buy a robot vacuum but have been put off by how much it can cost to get a good one, now is not a bad time to start looking. We’re now on the final day of Prime Day, and...

item.title: Xreal’s best USB-C smart glasses are $50 off, but not for long
item.description: If you’ve been curious about the emerging world of smart glasses, Prime Day is your chance to try some of the best models with a rare discount from Best Buy or directly from Xreal. The high-end...

item.title: Prime Day is almost over, but these are still the best Apple deals I’ve seen
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day is now in its final day, but whether you’re looking for a new pair of wireless earbuds or a smartwatch, there’s a good chance you’ll still find a discount. The Apple Watch...

item.title: It’s now the Arduino Uno Q’s turn to get a price hike
item.description: Arduino's Raspberry Pi-like microcomputer the Uno Q is joining many other devices in getting a price hike this week. The 2GB version of the Uno Q, which is powered by a Qualcomm Dragonwing QRB2210...

item.title: Fitbit’s Charge 6 and kid-friendly Ace LTE are much cheaper for Prime Day
item.description: Prime Day is making it much easier to pick up a Fitbit without spending more than you want. Both the Fitbit Charge 6 and Fitbit Ace LTE are on steep discounts today. Right now, you can buy the...

item.title: Europe Is Fed Up and Wants Its Own AI
item.description: It's a stretch to think that the continent can build a top-tier model, but it has an advantage: Donald Trump.

item.title: OpenAI’s Jalapeño chip is Big Tech’s spiciest move away from Nvidia
item.description: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending. OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built...

item.title: New Linux pedit COW Exploit Enables Root Access by Poisoning Cached Binaries
item.description: A flaw in the Linux kernel's traffic-control subsystem can let a local unprivileged user gain root on affected systems. CVE-2026-46331, nicknamed "pedit COW," is an out-of-bounds write in the...

item.title: Amazon Q Developer Flaw Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code via MCP Configs
item.description: A high-severity flaw in Amazon Q Developer let a malicious repository run commands and steal a developer's cloud credentials. The path was short: a developer opens the repo, trusts the workspace,...

item.title: Early Bird pricing ends tonight for TechCrunch Founder Summit
item.description: Save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026. Early Bird pricing ends today, at 11:59 p.m. PT, after which rates increase. Register now.
